acceptance_test: Every description of Lena's neural link port material uses the same term throughout the manuscript.
evidence: Ch 6 line 1032: 'two centimeters of surgical polymer'; Ch 10 line 1910: 'The implant's ceramic edge'; Ch 12 line 2324: 'a small circle of dark composite'; Ch 13 line 2581: 'two-centimeter circle of metal and ceramic'; Ch 18 line 3804: 'the small circle of titanium.'
problem: Lena's neural link port material is described as five different substances across five chapters: surgical polymer (Ch 6), ceramic (Ch 10), dark composite (Ch 12), metal and ceramic (Ch 13), and titanium (Ch 18). These are mutually contradictory — a single implant cannot be made of all five materials.
rewrite_direction: Choose one canonical material description (e.g., 'titanium and ceramic composite' would reconcile most variants) and standardize across all five chapters. Update Ch 6 line 1032, Ch 10 line 1910, Ch 12 line 2324, Ch 13 line 2581, and Ch 18 line 3804 to use the same material.

acceptance_test: Every reference to which wrist Margot's feed interface is on names the same wrist throughout the manuscript.
evidence: Ch 2 line 262: 'her feed interface on her right wrist'; Ch 10 line 1770: 'Margot strapped the feed interface to her left wrist'; Ch 12 line 2302: 'Margot's left hand had gone to her right wrist — to the feed interface' (right wrist); Ch 12 line 2360: 'the feed interface on her left wrist' (left wrist).
problem: Margot's feed interface switches wrists: right (Ch 2 line 262, Ch 12 line 2302), left (Ch 10 line 1770, Ch 12 line 2360). Ch 12 contradicts itself within the same chapter — line 2302 places it on the right wrist while line 2360 places it on the left. The device is personal equipment Margot wears consistently; it should be on the same wrist throughout.
rewrite_direction: Choose one canonical wrist (right, per the Ch 2 establishment) and correct all references. Update Ch 10 line 1770 and Ch 12 line 2360 from 'left' to 'right.' Verify Ch 12 line 2302 reads consistently.

acceptance_test: Ch 7 contains no double-hyphen sequences (' -- '); all dashes in Ch 7 are em-dashes (—) matching the other 17 chapters.
evidence: Ch 7 (lines 1127-1383) uses double-hyphens with spaces (' -- ') in 35 instances as em-dash substitutes. All other 17 chapters use proper em-dashes (—). Example: Ch 7 line 1129: 'The bone had knitted -- faster than any break had a right to mend.'
problem: Ch 7 is the only chapter using double-hyphens instead of em-dashes. This creates a visible formatting inconsistency when the manuscript is read as a compiled document. All 35 instances are in Ch 7; no other chapter exhibits this pattern.
rewrite_direction: Convert all 35 double-hyphen instances in Ch 7 from ' -- ' to '—' (em-dash) to match the rest of the manuscript.
